explanation: Among the eight core industries, natural gas output declined (negative growth) in 2015–16, while refinery products, fertilizer and coal grew. (An economics item.)

subject: Science
stem: As per the 2019–20 Budget Estimates, the maximum expenditure on major subsidies was likely on
explanation: The food subsidy (for the public distribution system/FCI) is consistently the largest of the major subsidies. (An economics item.)

subject: Science
stem: In medieval India, indigo extraction improved greatly as a result of
options: use of fertilizer to enhance crop quality
mechanical crushing tools for indigo stems
use of gypsum and lime mortars to make indigo vats water-proof
slave labour for desilting indigo crucibles
explanation: Lining the vats with gypsum/lime mortar made them water-tight, allowing controlled fermentation and better indigo yield. (A history item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which is not a nitrogen fertilizer?
explanation: Ammonium sulphate, ammonium nitrate and urea all supply nitrogen. The odd option (printed garbled as "NI") is the one lacking plant-available nitrogen, hence not a nitrogen fertilizer. (Please verify the intended option c against the original paper.)

subject: Science
stem: "If you feel you are trapped in a black hole, don't give up. There is a way out." Who said this?
explanation: The remark is from Stephen Hawking, the physicist renowned for his work on black holes.

subject: Science
stem: The Nobel Prize in Physics for 2016 was awarded to
explanation: The 2016 Physics Nobel was shared by Thouless, Haldane and Kosterlitz for theoretical discoveries of topological phases of matter.

subject: Science
stem: Bose-Einstein Condensate is
explanation: The Bose-Einstein Condensate (BEC) — formed at near-absolute-zero temperatures — is regarded as the fifth state of matter (after solid, liquid, gas and plasma).

subject: Science
stem: Who is not a recipient of the 2022 Nobel Prize in Physics?
explanation: The 2022 Physics Nobel went to Aspect, Clauser and Zeilinger (quantum entanglement). Klaus Hasselmann won in 2021 (climate modelling), so he is the odd one out.

subject: Science
stem: About science in medieval South India:
subs: 1. The Katapayadi numeral system was invented by Vararuchi of Kerala, author of Chandra Vakyas, to calculate the Moon's position.
2. Achyuta Pisharati wrote Uparagakriyakrama, a text on eclipses.
3. Ravi Varma of Mahodayapuram set up an observatory and inaugurated the Kollam era.
explanation: All three statements about Kerala's medieval astronomy/mathematics tradition are accepted as correct. (A history of science item.)

subject: Science
stem: Jammu and Kashmir Rifles is
options: an infantry regiment of the Indian Army
a battalion of the Rashtriya Rifles
the Armed Police of the State of J&K
a paramilitary force under the Ministry of Home Affairs
explanation: The Jammu and Kashmir Rifles is a regular infantry regiment of the Indian Army. (A defence item.)

subject: Science
stem: The chemical properties of an element depend upon
options: the number of electrons in the outermost shell
explanation: Valence (outermost-shell) electrons determine how an element bonds and reacts — hence its chemical properties.

subject: Science
stem: The rank of Captain in the Indian Navy is equivalent to
options: Group Captain in the Indian Air Force
Lieutenant Colonel of the Indian Army
Wing Commander of the Indian Air Force
explanation: A Navy Captain equals an Army Colonel and an Air Force Group Captain. (An Army Captain is a much junior rank.) (A defence item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which statement about the Indian Air Force is correct?
options: It has bases in many friendly countries.
It does not carry out joint exercises with any country.
It has a separate Maintenance Command.
No officer has ever been elevated to Marshal of the IAF.
explanation: The IAF has a dedicated Maintenance Command. It conducts joint exercises, and Arjan Singh was made Marshal of the IAF, so the other statements are false. (A defence item.)

subject: Science
stem: The main constituent of pearl is
explanation: A pearl is mostly calcium carbonate (aragonite/nacre) secreted by molluscs around an irritant.

subject: Science
stem: Most ozone gas (about 90%) is located in the
explanation: About 90% of atmospheric ozone lies in the stratosphere (the "ozone layer"), which absorbs harmful UV radiation. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: The theme of World Environment Day 2017 was
options: Green Economy – Does it include you?
Many Species, One Planet, One Future
explanation: "Connecting People to Nature" was the World Environment Day 2017 theme (hosted by Canada). (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which element is used as a timekeeper in atomic clocks?
explanation: Caesium-133 atomic transitions define the SI second and are used in the most precise atomic clocks.

subject: Science
stem: Which gas dissolves in water to give an acidic solution?
explanation: CO₂ dissolves to form carbonic acid (H₂CO₃), giving a weakly acidic solution.

subject: Science
stem: Which place in India experiences the highest atmospheric pressure during winter?
explanation: Cold, dense air sinks and raises surface pressure, so the cold high-altitude desert of Leh experiences high winter pressure. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: Ascending order of these atmospheric gases by volume percentage:
subs: 1. Argon
2. Neon
3. Helium
4. Carbon dioxide.
explanation: By volume in dry air: Helium (~0.0005%) < Neon (~0.0018%) < CO₂ (~0.04%) < Argon (~0.93%), giving the order 3-2-4-1. None of the printed options reproduces this exactly, so the option set appears to be a transcription error.

subject: Science
stem: The RBI's high-level task force on the Public Credit Registry was headed by
explanation: The RBI task force on the Public Credit Registry (2017) was chaired by Y. M. Deosthalee. (An economics item.)

subject: Science
stem: Around the 12th century, Sufi Silsilas began to crystallize to signify:
subs: 1. continuous link between master and disciple
2. unbroken spiritual genealogy to the Prophet Muhammad
3. transmission of spiritual power and blessings to devotees.
explanation: A Silsila (Sufi order) embodied all three — the master–disciple chain, an unbroken spiritual lineage to the Prophet, and transmission of spiritual blessings. (A history item.)

subject: Science
stem: The theme of World Soil Day 2017 was
options: Soils and pulses, a symbol for life
Caring for the Planet starts from the Ground
Soils, a solid ground for life
explanation: "Caring for the Planet starts from the Ground" was the World Soil Day 2017 theme. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Science
stem: About the Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP):
subs: 1. Raised in 1962.
2. Basically a trained mountain force.
3. Replaced Assam Rifles in Sikkim and Arunachal in 2004–05 for border guarding.
4. Presently has 52 service battalions.
explanation: The ITBP was raised in 1962 (1), is a specialised mountain force (2), and took over Sikkim/Arunachal border guarding around 2004–05 (3). The exact battalion count in statement 4 is the doubtful figure. (Worth confirming against the official key.)

subject: Science
stem: Which characteristic is common to hydrogen, nitrogen, oxygen and carbon dioxide?
options: They are all gases at room temperature.
They all have the same reactivity.
explanation: H₂, N₂, O₂ and CO₂ are all gases at room temperature. (CO₂ is triatomic, and they are colourless with very different reactivities.)

subject: Science
stem: Who is the Convener of the Task Force (set up Nov 2017) to review the Income-tax Act and draft a new direct tax law?
explanation: Arbind Modi (CBDT member) was the convener of the 2017 direct-tax-law task force. (An economics item.)

subject: Science
stem: The two defence industrial corridors announced in the 2018 Budget are coming up in
explanation: The two Defence Industrial Corridors are in Uttar Pradesh and Tamil Nadu. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Science
stem: Regarding the procedure and conduct of business in Parliament, which is not correct?
options: To discuss police force used in suppressing SC/ST communities
To discuss violent disturbances in a Union-controlled Undertaking
To discuss putting down demands of industrial labour
explanation: Parliamentary devices (like adjournment motions) address matters of urgent national importance within Union jurisdiction; purely State subjects are generally outside their scope. (The question is poorly transcribed and is a polity item; confirm against the original paper.)

subject: Science
stem: Very small insoluble particles in a liquid may be separated by
explanation: Centrifugation spins the mixture at high speed so fine suspended particles settle out — used for very small insoluble particles (e.g. separating blood components).

subject: Science
stem: Which planet of our solar system has the least mass?
explanation: Mercury is the smallest and least massive planet in the solar system. (Astronomy.)

subject: Science
stem: What is a constellation?
options: A pattern of equidistant stars in the sky
A pattern of stars that may not be equidistant from the Earth
A pattern of planets in the sky
A pattern of stars, planets and satellites
explanation: A constellation is an apparent pattern of stars; the stars only look grouped — in reality they lie at very different distances from Earth. (Astronomy.)

subject: Science
stem: The Public Financial Management System (PFMS) is designed, developed, owned and implemented by the
options: Institute of Government Accounts and Finance
explanation: PFMS is run by the Office of the Controller General of Accounts (Ministry of Finance) for tracking fund flows and DBT. (An economics item.)

subject: Science
stem: The National Dope Testing Laboratory functions under the
options: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports
explanation: The NDTL operates under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. (A governance item.)

subject: Science
stem: Consider:
subs: 1. Particles of matter intermix on their own.
2. Particles of matter have force acting between them.
explanation: Both are fundamental properties of matter — particles diffuse/intermix on their own (e.g. perfume in air) and exert intermolecular forces of attraction.

subject: Science
stem: Marsilea, Fern and Horse-tail are examples of which plant group?
explanation: Ferns, Marsilea and horsetails (Equisetum) are pteridophytes — seedless vascular plants that reproduce by spores. (A biology item.)

subject: Science
stem: Scattering of α-particles by a thin gold foil suggests the presence of
options: a positively charged nucleus at the centre of an atom
explanation: Rutherford's experiment showed a tiny, dense, positively charged nucleus at the atom's centre.

subject: Science
stem: The unit of the force constant k of a spring is
explanation: From Hooke's law F = kx, k = F/x has units of newton per metre (N/m).

subject: Science
stem: Blowing air from the mouth into limewater turns it milky due to the presence of
explanation: Exhaled air contains CO₂, which reacts with limewater [Ca(OH)₂] to form insoluble calcium carbonate, turning it milky.

subject: Science
stem: The Vijaynagar Advanced Landing Ground of the IAF, recently reopened, is located in
explanation: The Vijaynagar ALG is in Arunachal Pradesh, near the India–Myanmar frontier. (A geography/defence item.)

subject: Science
stem: Formulation of policy on Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs) is the responsibility of
options: the Ministry of Law and Justice
the Department of Science and Technology
the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
the Ministry of Human Resource Development
explanation: IPR policy in India is handled by the DPIIT (under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry). (A governance item.)

subject: Science
stem: What is "Little Boy"?
options: The fission bomb dropped at Hiroshima
The fusion bomb dropped at Nagasaki
The first nuclear bomb tested by America
The first nuclear bomb tested by North Korea
explanation: "Little Boy" was the uranium fission bomb dropped on Hiroshima in August 1945. (A history item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which statement about Gupta coins is not correct?
options: The Guptas issued large numbers of gold coins called Dinar.
Chandragupta II, Kumaragupta I, Skandagupta and Budhagupta issued silver coins.
Obverses bear images of kings, reverses bear deities.
The largest number of coins issued by the Guptas were of copper.
explanation: The Guptas are famous for their gold coins, not copper; in fact relatively few copper coins were issued — so statement d is false. (A history item.)

subject: Science
stem: If the distance between two objects is increased two times, the gravitational force between them will
explanation: Gravitational force follows the inverse-square law (F ∝ 1/r²); doubling the distance reduces the force to 1/4.

subject: Science
stem: Which statement about neutron properties is not correct?
options: Neutron mass is almost equal to proton mass.
Neutrons are located inside the atomic nuclei.
Neutrons revolve around the atomic nuclei.
explanation: Neutrons reside inside the nucleus, not orbiting it (electrons orbit the nucleus) — so statement d is false.

subject: Science
stem: Which does not form an oxide on reaction with oxygen?
explanation: Silver is highly unreactive toward oxygen and does not readily form an oxide, unlike magnesium, lead and tin.

subject: Science
stem: KIRAN (Knowledge Involvement in Research Advancement through Nurturing) provides opportunities to
options: women scientists who had a career break due to family reasons
explanation: KIRAN supports women scientists returning to research after a career break. (A governance item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which statement about the Coriolis force is not correct?
options: It is maximum at the Poles.
It is absent at the Equator.
It deflects wind to the right in the southern hemisphere.
It deflects wind to the right in the northern hemisphere.
explanation: In the southern hemisphere the Coriolis force deflects winds to the LEFT (to the right only in the northern hemisphere) — so statement c is false. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: The ozone layer, which absorbs ultraviolet radiation, is found in which atmospheric layer?
explanation: The ozone layer lies in the stratosphere, shielding the surface from harmful UV. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: Alluvial soils (sandy loam to clay) are generally
options: poor in potash, rich in phosphorus
poor in both potash and phosphorus
rich in potash and poor in phosphorus
explanation: Alluvial soils are generally rich in potash but poor in phosphorus (and nitrogen/humus). (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which is correct about Pinaka?
options: It is a multi-barrel rocket system.
It is an indigenous drone system.
explanation: Pinaka is an indigenous multi-barrel rocket launcher. (A defence item.)

subject: Science
stem: The Peninsula Shield Force is the military arm of the
explanation: The Peninsula Shield Force is the joint military arm of the GCC (Gulf states). (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which metal is kept immersed in kerosene oil to protect it and prevent accidental fire?
explanation: Sodium reacts violently with air and water, so it is stored under kerosene to keep it away from moisture and oxygen.

subject: Science
stem: Of three copper wires of different lengths and cross-sections, which has the highest resistivity?
options: All wires have the same resistivity
explanation: Resistivity is an intrinsic material property — it depends only on the material (copper) and temperature, not on length or cross-section. So all three have the same resistivity.

subject: Science
stem: Slaked lime used for whitewashing reacts with component A in air to form a thin layer of component B (shiny finish). A and B are
explanation: Slaked lime [Ca(OH)₂] reacts with atmospheric CO₂ (A) to form calcium carbonate, CaCO₃ (B), which gives walls their hard, shiny white finish.

subject: Science
stem: The weight of an object is due to
options: the net force acting on it
the total of all forces acting on it irrespective of direction
the force it exerts on the ground
explanation: Strictly, weight is the gravitational force exerted by the Earth on the object (W = mg) — none of the options states this cleanly. Among the choices, the gravitational pull is intended; the option wording is imprecise, so this item should be checked against the official key.

subject: Science
stem: Which material is present in a guard tube (drying tube) used in the preparation of HCl gas?
explanation: Anhydrous calcium chloride is a drying agent that absorbs moisture, keeping the HCl gas dry in the guard tube.

subject: Science
stem: Inceptisols, Entisols, Vertisols and Mollisols are orders of
explanation: These are soil orders in the USDA soil-taxonomy classification. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which is a typical example of monopolistic competition?
explanation: Monopolistic competition features many sellers offering differentiated (branded) products — the soap market (many brands) is the classic example. (An economics item.)

subject: Science
stem: What happens to the gravitational force between two objects if the mass of one is doubled and the distance is also doubled?
options: The force increases by a factor of 4
explanation: F ∝ m/r². Doubling the mass multiplies F by 2; doubling the distance divides F by 4. Net effect: 2/4 = ½, so the force is halved.

subject: Science
stem: Which land surface has the highest albedo?
explanation: Fresh snow reflects most incoming sunlight (highest albedo, ~80–90%), far more than rock, sand or vegetation. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: The global initiative known as the "30 x 30 target" refers to
options: At least 30% of the planet protected by 2030
30% of cultivated area under organic farming by 2030
30% of the poor lifted out of poverty by 2030
explanation: The "30x30" biodiversity goal (Kunming-Montreal framework) aims to protect at least 30% of land and oceans by 2030. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Science
stem: Travelling by the shortest road from Bikaner to Konark, which vegetation sequence would you find?
options: Tropical thorny, Desert, Moist deciduous, Mangrove
Desert, Tropical thorny, Moist deciduous, Mangrove
Moist deciduous, Tropical thorny, Mangrove, Desert
Desert, Tropical thorny, Mangrove, Moist deciduous
explanation: Going from arid Bikaner (desert) eastward across the thorny scrub, into the wetter moist deciduous belt, and finally the coastal mangroves near Konark (Odisha). (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which pair of metals are very good conductors of heat?
explanation: Silver and copper are the two best metallic conductors of heat (and electricity). Lead and mercury are relatively poor conductors.

subject: Science
stem: The Government is promoting Kisan Drones for:
subs: 1. Spraying weedicides.
2. Digitisation of land records.
3. Crop assessment.
explanation: Kisan Drones are promoted for crop assessment (3) and digitization of land records (2), along with spraying of nutrients/insecticides. The phrasing "spraying weedicides" (1) is the doubtful element. (Worth confirming against the official key.)

subject: Science
stem: Storage of biological tissues (blood, organs, semen) at low temperature is achieved using
explanation: Liquid nitrogen (−196 °C) is the standard cryogenic medium for preserving biological tissues.

subject: Science
stem: Which countries were recently placed in the Grey List of the Financial Action Task Force?
explanation: Turkey and the UAE were added to the FATF "grey list" for increased monitoring (2022). (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Science
stem: The terms Ethereum, Solana, Polkadot and Tether refer to
explanation: All four are cryptocurrencies/blockchain platforms. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Science
stem: About a mobile phone's microphone and speaker:
subs: 1. The microphone converts sound to a mechanical signal.
2. The microphone converts sound to an electrical signal.
3. The speaker converts a mechanical signal to sound.
4. The speaker converts an electrical signal to sound.
explanation: A microphone converts sound into an electrical signal (2) and a speaker converts an electrical signal back into sound (4).

subject: Science
stem: On burning a magnesium ribbon in air:
subs: 1. White powder of MgO is formed.
2. It is a combustion reaction.
3. Heat and light are produced.
explanation: Burning magnesium gives a dazzling flame (heat and light), is a combustion (oxidation) reaction, and forms white magnesium oxide (MgO) — all three are correct.

subject: Science
stem: Which is the only paramilitary force with a dual control structure?
explanation: The Assam Rifles has a dual control structure — administrative control under the Ministry of Home Affairs and operational control under the Indian Army (Ministry of Defence). (A defence item.)

subject: Science
stem: What percentage of carbon dioxide is present in the atmosphere of Venus and Mars?
explanation: Both Venus and Mars have atmospheres that are about 95–97% carbon dioxide. (Astronomy.)

subject: Science
stem: Which method is used to separate the constituents present in a compound?
explanation: A compound can only be broken into its elements by chemical means such as electrolysis (an electrochemical method) — physical methods like distillation only separate mixtures.

subject: Science
stem: During whitewashing, slaked lime reacts slowly with CO₂ to form a thin layer of calcium carbonate. Which reaction represents this correctly?
explanation: Whitewashing uses slaked lime, Ca(OH)₂, which reacts with atmospheric CO₂ to deposit hard CaCO₃ plus water — option d with the correct states.

subject: Science
stem: A pendulum is shown at positions P, T, Q, S, R. Which statement is true?
options: Minimum potential energy at P and T
Minimum potential energy at Q and S
Same potential energy at all positions
explanation: Potential energy is lowest at the lowest point of the swing (the mean/rest position, shown as R), where kinetic energy is maximum. (Depends on the figure; R is the bottom of the arc.)

subject: Science
stem: Identify the soil: large proportion of sodium, potassium and magnesium making it infertile; structure sandy to loamy; widespread in western Gujarat, eastern-coast deltas and the Sundarbans.
explanation: High salt (sodium) content, coastal/delta distribution and infertility point to saline (alkaline) soils. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: About planets:
subs: 1. Mercury has no atmosphere.
2. Venus has two moons.
3. There is no land on Jupiter.
4. Saturn's rings are composed of lumps of ice and dust.
explanation: Mercury has essentially no atmosphere (1), Jupiter is a gas giant with no solid surface (3), and Saturn's rings are ice and dust (4). Venus has no moons (not two), so statement 2 is false. (Astronomy.)

subject: Science
stem: Which is related to global dimming?
options: Fall of atmospheric pressure due to increased particulates
Rise of pressure due to particulates
Rise of temperature due to particulates
Fall of temperature due to increased particulates
explanation: Global dimming is the drop in surface temperature/sunlight caused by aerosols and particulates that reflect and scatter incoming solar radiation. (An environment item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which biosphere reserve is spread over three States in India?
explanation: The Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve spans Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Karnataka. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: To cool a bowl of freshly cooked rice quickly, the best option is to
options: keep it in a room with no fan/AC
keep it in an AC room (~23 °C) with a fan at slow speed
keep it in a bowl of water at room temperature with a fan at full speed
keep it in a bowl of water at room temperature only
explanation: Cooling is fastest when heat is removed by both conduction into surrounding water and rapid evaporation/convection driven by a fast fan — combining the two mechanisms in option c.

subject: Science
stem: The two isotopes of elemental chlorine are
explanation: Natural chlorine is a mix of two isotopes, chlorine-35 and chlorine-37 (giving the average atomic mass ≈ 35.5). The option subscripts/superscripts did not survive text extraction.

subject: Science
stem: Which statement is not true for glass?
options: Sodium silicate is known as water glass.
Glass is also called a supercooled liquid.
Crystallinity develops on cooling of the molten glass.
Coloured glasses can be made by adding colouring materials.
explanation: Glass is an amorphous (non-crystalline) solid — it does not develop crystallinity on cooling — so statement c is false. The others are correct.

subject: Science
stem: The Financial Action Task Force is
options: A wing under the Central Economic Intelligence Bureau
A division of the Enforcement Directorate
A global money-laundering and terrorist-financing watchdog
explanation: The FATF is the inter-governmental body that sets standards to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. (A current-affairs item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which statement about INS Tarmugli is not correct?
options: It is a fast attack craft commissioned by the Indian Navy.
It was commissioned at Naval Dockyard, Visakhapatnam.
It was given to the Sri Lankan Naval Force in 2006 and returned to the Indian Navy in May 2023.
It is to be used for coastal surveillance.
explanation: INS Tarmugli is an indigenous fast attack craft commissioned for the Indian Navy (used for coastal surveillance); the claim that it was gifted to Sri Lanka in 2006 and returned in 2023 (c) is the incorrect statement. (A defence item worth confirming against the official key.)

subject: Science
stem: Identify the mineral: usually a greenish crystal; often found in basaltic rocks; magnesium, iron and silica are its major elements.
explanation: Olivine is a greenish magnesium-iron silicate common in basaltic (mafic) rocks. (A geology item. Note: a separate, garbled "S-waves" statement appears merged with this item in the source.)

subject: Science
stem: About salt weathering:
subs: 1. Salt crystallisation is the most effective of all salt-weathering processes.
2. Salt-crystal growth is more effective over sedimentary rocks than igneous rocks.
explanation: Salt crystallisation is indeed the most effective salt-weathering process, and it works better on porous sedimentary rocks (which absorb saline water) than on dense igneous rocks. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: Correct ascending order of these salts by their presence in sea water:
subs: 1. Calcium Carbonate
2. Calcium Sulphate
3. Magnesium Chloride.
explanation: By abundance in seawater: Calcium carbonate (least) < Calcium sulphate < Magnesium chloride (most) — so ascending order is 1, 2, 3. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which Biosphere Reserve is spread over three states in India?
explanation: The Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve extends across Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Karnataka. (A geography item.)

subject: Science
stem: A body floats in a liquid if the buoyant force is
explanation: A floating body is in equilibrium when the upward buoyant force equals its weight (Archimedes' principle).

subject: Science
stem: A metallic sphere of mass 1 kg and volume 2 × 10⁻⁴ m³ is fully immersed in water (density 1000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s²). The buoyant force is
explanation: Buoyant force = ρ_water × V × g = 1000 × 2 × 10⁻⁴ × 10 = 2 N. (Volume taken as 2 × 10⁻⁴ m³; the exponent was garbled in the source.)

subject: Science
stem: The force with which the Earth attracts an object is called
explanation: The weight of an object is the gravitational force with which the Earth attracts it (W = mg).

subject: Science
stem: Isobars of elements have the same
explanation: Isobars have the same mass number but different atomic numbers (e.g. ⁴⁰Ca and ⁴⁰Ar).

subject: Science
stem: Which is considered a compound?
explanation: Calcium carbonate (CaCO₃) is a compound (elements chemically combined in fixed ratio). Silicon and tin are elements; a sugar solution is a mixture.

subject: Science
stem: "Since wars begin in the minds of men, it is in the minds of men that the defences of peace must be constructed." This appears in the Constitution of which UN organ?
explanation: This line is from the Preamble to the UNESCO Constitution. (A current-affairs/IR item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which is not an application software?
explanation: A linker is a system/utility program (it combines object code into an executable), not an application. Spreadsheets, AutoCAD and PowerPoint are application software. (An IT item.)

subject: Science
stem: Which metal is widely extracted by the electrolysis of its molten compound?
explanation: Highly reactive metals like sodium are extracted by electrolysis of their molten salts (e.g. molten NaCl in the Down's process), since they cannot be reduced by ordinary chemical means.

subject: Science
stem: About the PLFS report 2023-24:
subs: 1. Agriculture's employment share rose from about 44% (2017-18) to about 46% (2023-24).
2. The share of female workers in agriculture increased over 2017-18 to 2023-24.
explanation: Both statements are correct — the agriculture share of employment rose and the share of female workers in agriculture increased. (The options referencing "3" and "4" are spurious, as only two statements are given.) (An economics item.)

subject: Science
stem: What are the uses of software ports?
subs: 1. Connect client computer to server.
2. Connect external devices to computer.
3. Identify different services like email, file transfer.
4. Connecting peripherals like cameras, scanners.
explanation: Software (logical/network) ports connect a client to a server and identify services (statements 1 and 3). Connecting external devices/peripherals (2, 4) is the job of hardware ports. Among the given options (which reference only statements 1 and 2), statement 1 alone is correct. (Options appear truncated in the source.)

subject: Science
stem: In a 4-bit computer, what result is produced after subtracting 3 from 5 using 2's complement representation?
explanation: [Defective option set] In 4-bit two's complement, 5 = 0101 and -3 = 1101; 0101 + 1101 = 1 0010. A 4-bit machine discards the carry, so the stored result is 0010 = 2. Since 0010 is not among the listed options, the question is defective.

subject: Science
stem: Which surveys are conducted by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation?
subs: 1. Survey on Social Consumption: Health.
2. Comprehensive Modular Survey: Telecom and ICT skills.
3. Periodic Labour Force Survey.
4. Annual Survey on Unincorporated Enterprises.
explanation: All four are surveys conducted by MoSPI/NSO (health consumption, telecom-ICT modular survey, PLFS and the unincorporated-enterprises survey). (An economics item.)

subject: Science
stem: About Arnala, an Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft:
subs: 1. It is indigenously designed in India.
2. It is named after the historic Arnala fort in Maharashtra.
3. It was built under a Public-Private Partnership of GRSE with M/s L&T Shipyard.
explanation: All three are correct — INS Arnala is indigenously designed, named after the Arnala fort (Maharashtra), and built under a GRSE–L&T public-private partnership. (A defence item.)
